title: Fixes in Imagenet training script
body:
* ngpus_per_node variable count fix
* dist_backend assertion added for nccl issue

step_plan.summary: Update imagenet/main.py to compute the actual GPU count (ngpus_per_node) without forcing a fallback when CUDA is unavailable, and add defensive checks to disallow selecting the 'nccl' backend when CUDA/GPU is not present, surfacing a clear error message.

steps.operation: Fix ngpus_per_node computation and add dist-backend assertion in argument/init section
steps.reason: Ensure ngpus_per_node reflects the actual number of CUDA devices (0 if none) instead of forcing a default of 1 when CUDA is unavailable. Also detect misconfiguration early by preventing selection of the 'nccl' distributed backend when CUDA/GPU support is not available, giving a clear error message to the user.
steps.side_effects: Scripts running on CPU-only machines will now have ngpus_per_node == 0, which will cause early failure or require explicit handling by callers; this prevents silent incorrect behavior. Tests that assumed a CPU fallback (ngpus_per_node==1) will need updating. Any logic that spawns processes (mp.spawn) will now observe the true GPU count; CI jobs or downstream tools that expect spawning on CPU may need change. No API change beyond clearer error messages; users who previously relied on the implicit fallback must adjust flags or enable CUDA. This change reduces obscure NCCL runtime errors by making configuration errors explicit.

steps.operation: Add defensive check before initializing distributed process group to block NCCL without CUDA
steps.reason: Before calling torch.distributed.init_process_group (or equivalent), verify that if args.dist_backend == 'nccl' then CUDA is available (or ngpus_per_node > 0). This prevents runtime failures from trying to initialize NCCL when no GPU/CUDA is present and provides a clear, actionable error message (or allows falling back to 'gloo').
steps.side_effects: Will raise an explicit error earlier if the user attempts to use NCCL on CPU-only systems, improving debuggability. Tests or scripts that previously attempted NCCL on non-GPU environments must be updated to use 'gloo' or enable CUDA. Downstream initializations dependent on the process group will not be attempted if the check fails; no API changes beyond clearer failure semantics.
